name: Central Clemson Recreation Center
description: Come play pickleball at Central Clemson Recreation Center in Central, SC! There are 9 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: Dacusville Rec Department
description: Dacusville Rec Department is a place to play pickleball in Easley, SC. There is 1 outdoor asphalt court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Players have access to lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Hagood Park
description: These courts are dual tennis/pickleball courts. One court has the net lowered to correct pickleball height the other is set at tennis height.

name: Nettles Park
description: Nettles Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Clemson, SC. There are 12 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. The courts cannot be reserved. Amenities include water, lights, and restrooms.

name: Pope Field Park
description: Come play pickleball at Pope Field Park in Easley, SC! There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Smithfields Country Club
description: Members may bring a non-member guest for a 5.00 Fee.
fees: Members only — Members may bring a guest for a 5.00 fee.

name: The Reserve At Lake Keowee
description: The Reserve At Lake Keowee is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sunset, SC. There are 8 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, locker rooms, water, and lights. A membership is required to play.
